comments.author: @TuyetPham-n6t
comments.text: I visited St. Thomas on a cruise a few years ago, and this video brought back so many amazing memories.

comments.author: @christophertaylor7006
comments.text: …sounds far better than the know, niice words, though!…

comments.author: @scorporsupremacy7948
comments.text: Born and raised in St.Thomas 🇻🇮 with near 6 decades under my belt. I found several things in this video to be inaccurate. Most of what is being said does not go with the imagery used in this documentary. Certain foods shown in this video are not local/traditional foods eaten here. We don’t eat horseshoe crabs here and we don’t have freshwater fish here to eat. Yet this video is showing certain species of fish being cooked that are not available here. There are no garage style roll up doors used on Main Streets to open the stores. Everything shown here were repetitive clips of the tourists spots from different angles. Not one local scene was provided except for the Market Square. This video just doesn’t depict St.Thomas accurately in my opinion.

comments.author: @TKO67
comments.text: 15:23 CRINGE !!!! It is not pronounced Fun Guy. It is pronounced. FoonGee. It is obvious the speaker never has been to the Caribbean at all.

comments.author: @JackSteven-q2m
comments.text: The contrast between the pristine Caribbean paradise and the raw, hardworking daily life of the locals is perfectly captured here. The vintage cinematic style and slow pans make this 4K documentary feel deeply authentic and grounded. A beautiful tribute to St. Thomas.

comments.author: @Emelibeg
comments.text: The intimate depth of field in these 4K shots is remarkable. It truly isolates the genuine soul of St. Thomas from the crowded tourist spots. Capturing the authentic daily routines of the locals with such a vintage cinematic feel is a beautiful achievement.

comments.text: What about the high crime that I keep hearing about.

comments.author: @RemoteEarthJourneys
comments.text: *Living in St. Thomas feels like seeing the Caribbean from a completely different perspective. The beaches are stunning, but what impressed me most is how strong the sense of family and community still is. Would you trade city life for this island lifestyle? 🌴🌊*

comments.text: Been there 3 Times I love Coki point and carnival

comments.author: @Jim-Brown-g4w
comments.text: I visited Saint Thomas about 20 years ago and loved it !

comments.author: @GlobeExperiences2026
comments.text: St. Thomas is the ultimate tropical dream! 🇻🇮 This 4K travel documentary offers such a realistic and vibrant look at living in St. Thomas and the US Virgin Islands. The idea of living in an American Caribbean paradise with no passport needed for US citizens is a massive draw for expats and retirees. From the crystal-clear waters of Magens Bay to everyday island life around Charlotte Amalie, this video balances the breathtaking beauty with genuine insights on island time and living costs. Incredible footage!

comments.author: @rfr7039
comments.text: Born and raised in STT, Left in 1985. come back home occasionally.

comments.author: @travelnearme
comments.text: St. Thomas is not just a tropical paradise, but a place where family, community, and genuine resilience shape daily life. It makes me wonder what it would feel like to watch the sunset there after the cruise ships have left. Has anyone here ever truly experienced St. Thomas beyond the popular tourist spots. I would love to visit someday and see that authentic side for myself.

replies.author: @Jigganva
replies.text: There is a window in August that’s quieter because all the cruises ships are in Alaska!!  I stayed for 7 days during this time and got to see the island for what it is, paradise!!  Only drawback is the restaurants closed early for a vacation spot!!  The beaches on St. John was some of the most beautiful in the world.

comments.author: @EpicAdventureWorld
comments.text: If you had one free week in the Caribbean, would you spend all of it in St. Thomas or island-hop? I'd love to hear everyone's answer.

replies.author: @CarltonPhilip
replies.text: Island hop

comments.author: @mejoresviajes6688
comments.text: This video beautifully captures the vibrant culture, daily life, and breathtaking Caribbean scenery of St. Thomas. Would you choose to live in this tropical paradise or only visit for a vacation?

comments.author: @lastlivingplaces
comments.text: This offers such a refreshing perspective on Argentina. The vintage aesthetic and slow travel pacing highlight the real, everyday culture beyond the stadiums. It feels incredibly genuine, showing the true heart of the local people.

comments.text: I lived and taught in the St.Thomas for over forty years I appreciate the fact that I was able to see familiar places. I live here in St. Thomas. 
Loved the cinematography, showed a lot of Charlotte Amalie and what tourist see. But not quite an accurate depiction what daily life is like. That being said, I wouldn't trade it for anywhere else. ✌️😎🏝

replies.author: @RemoteEarthJourneys
replies.text: That’s the interesting part about places like St. Thomas—visitors often fall in love with the scenery, while locals know the real story behind island life. Even with all the challenges, it says a lot that you still wouldn’t trade it for anywhere else. 🌴😊

replies.author: @scorporsupremacy7948
replies.text: You sound very childish and ignorant. Just so you know, many St.Lucians live here and call here home. There kids are born here and getting an education here. Some of your bad St.Lucians are living here because they fled St.Lucia for crimes they committed there. So don’t talk as though St.Lucia is the best. No place on this earth is perfect and all its people have their flaws. This constant beckoning about whose island is better than the other is what keeps many of us distant from trying to live in harmony. So quit being a salop.

I've got to say St Lucia is one of my least favourite of all the caribbean Islands and I've visited 11. That said, for anyone who hasn't been to St Lucia, I would recommend they do.
